evals.name: pre-launch-full-workspace-email-bison
evals.expected_output: A/B variants for all 4 email positions meeting 3/2/2/2 minimums, with descriptive labels, consistent tone within positions, verification gate checklist, and Email Bison spintax
expectations: E1 (The Pitch) has at least 3 body variants including the control
E2 (The Nudge) has at least 2 body variants including the control
E3 (The Pivot) has at least 2 body variants including the control
E4 (The Breakup) has at least 2 body variants including the control
Total step count is 9 or more
Every variant has a descriptive label (e.g. 'Pain Dagger Opener', 'Social Proof Opener')
Variants within the same position change the approach/frame but keep tone and approximate length consistent
Email Bison spintax format used in all variants: {option1|option2|option3} NOT {{RANDOM | ...}}
Verification gate checklist is present with all positions checked off
Subject line variants provided (at least 2-3 per position)
Each variant clearly identifies which test variable it changes (angle, hook, proof point, value lever)
No em dashes or en dashes in any variant
{SENDER_EMAIL_SIGNATURE} token present in every variant
No spam trigger words (free, guarantee, act now, limited time, risk-free)

evals.name: pre-launch-bare-minimum-user-copy
evals.expected_output: Variants generated from user-provided copy without requiring a workspace or strategy file, meeting 3/2/2/2 minimums
expectations: Does NOT refuse or ask for a strategy file before generating variants
E1 has at least 3 body variants
E2 has at least 2 body variants
E3 has at least 2 body variants
E4 has at least 2 body variants
Total steps >= 9
Variants have descriptive labels
Control copy is preserved as one of the variants (labeled as control)
New variants change the approach/angle, not just rephrase the same words
Verification gate checklist included and all positions pass
Email Bison spintax format used
No spam trigger words in any variant

evals.name: pre-launch-instantly-spintax-format
evals.expected_output: Variants using Instantly spintax format ({{RANDOM | ...}}) with proper {{firstName}} tokens, meeting minimums
expectations: E1 has at least 3 body variants
E2 has at least 2 body variants
Total steps >= 9
Instantly spintax format used: {{RANDOM | option1 | option2 | option3}} NOT {option1|option2|option3}
Personalization token is {{firstName}} (Instantly format) in every variant
Variants have descriptive labels
Verification gate checklist present and passing
No em dashes or en dashes
Subject line variants provided for unthreaded positions
No spam trigger words
E1 variants test different angles (pain vs proof vs curiosity) not just word swaps

evals.name: post-launch-winner-analysis-sufficient-data
evals.prompt:
Analyze A/B test results for our brightsmile-dental campaign on Email Bison.

Campaign analytics:
E1 Variant A (Pain Dagger): 520 sent, 18 replies (3.46%)
E1 Variant B (Social Proof): 490 sent, 8 replies (1.63%)
E1 Variant C (Curiosity Hook): 505 sent, 12 replies (2.38%)

E2 Variant A (Data Bump): 380 sent, 6 replies (1.58%)
E2 Variant B (Question Bump): 375 sent, 9 replies (2.40%)

E3 Variant A (Cost Savings): 290 sent, 7 replies (2.41%)
E3 Variant B (Time Savings): 285 sent, 3 replies (1.05%)

E4 Variant A (Redirect Close): 200 sent, 2 replies (1.00%)
E4 Variant B (Humor Close): 195 sent, 4 replies (2.05%)

Bounce rate: 1.8% overall. Warmup scores: all senders 96+. Campaign running for 14 days.
evals.expected_output: Statistical analysis with z-test results, winner declarations or trending status per position, specific actionable recommendations with data
expectations: Z-test or statistical significance calculation performed for each position's variant comparison
E1: Variant A (Pain Dagger) identified as leader at 3.46% vs 1.63% and 2.38%
Each position gets a status: winner declared, trending, equivalent, or insufficient data
Minimum 100 sends per variant requirement checked and noted
Specific recommendations name the exact variant labels and campaign
Recommendations include actual numbers (e.g. '3.46% reply rate after 520 sends')
Recommendations state a concrete action (e.g. 'deactivate Variant B, scale Variant A')
Bounce rate assessed against benchmarks (1.8% = healthy)
Warmup scores assessed (96+ = safe)
Cross-campaign pattern insights attempted (opening style, CTA type, subject format)
No generic advice like 'improve your subject lines' — every recommendation is specific
Performance benchmarks table referenced (reply rate: >= 3% excellent, >= 1% healthy)

evals.name: post-launch-insufficient-data-edge-case
evals.prompt:
Check which variant is winning for our new campaign consulti-agencies on Instantly.

Analytics so far:
E1 Variant A (RLM Sequences): 45 sent, 3 replies (6.67%)
E1 Variant B (Pain Dagger): 42 sent, 1 reply (2.38%)

E2 Variant A (Data Bump): 28 sent, 1 reply (3.57%)
E2 Variant B (New Context): 30 sent, 0 replies (0%)

E3 and E4: not yet reached (campaign is 4 days old).

Bounce rate: 2.1%. Warmup scores: 94-97 range.
evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies insufficient data for statistical analysis, does NOT declare winners, recommends continuing and provides volume needed
expectations: Does NOT declare a winner for any position (all below 100 sends threshold)
Explicitly states the 100 sends minimum requirement is not met
Notes E1 Variant A is trending/leading but NOT significant
Does NOT recommend deactivating any variant yet
Recommends continuing the test and estimates sends needed for significance
E3 and E4 correctly noted as not yet reached (no data)
Bounce rate 2.1% assessed as healthy (under 3%)
Warmup scores assessed (94-97 = safe to continue)
No premature optimization recommendations
Advises patience with specific guidance (e.g. 'continue for 7+ more days' or 'need ~55 more sends per E1 variant')
